environment.note: This is a linter-behavior check (does a diagnostic fire, yes/no), not a timing benchmark. The decisive precondition is that `typescript` is NOT installed in node_modules — the type-aware rules must infer types on their own. CPU/RAM do not affect pass/fail, so they are omitted. Speed numbers (Biome's cited 10-20x over ESLint) are NOT measured here; they are machine-dependent and only cited in the post.

subject.name: @biomejs/biome
subject.note: noFloatingPromises is lint/nursery/noFloatingPromises, domain `types`, available since 2.0.0 — still nursery (opt-in) as of 2.5.4, not in the recommended set.

baseline.name: typescript-eslint (typed-linting)
baseline.note: Compared conceptually in the post: what Biome's own type inference catches vs what typescript-eslint's full type-checker catches. No typescript-eslint code was executed in this run; the coverage gap (EXP3-B, a hand-rolled thenable) is a firsthand observation of Biome, and the comparison to typescript-eslint's behavior is cited from Biome's own '~75% of typescript-eslint's type-aware rules' framing, not measured.

raw_artifacts.retention_policy: none discarded — all evidence for this run is small and committed
raw_artifacts.note: The harness installs @biomejs/biome into a throwaway work/ dir (node_modules, ~2 packages) which run.sh recreates and which is .gitignored — it is not committed. The deterministic evidence — probe-result.json (the four pass/fail diagnostics) — is committed. Fixed fixtures produce fixed diagnostics, so a re-run reproduces the same booleans on the pinned Biome version.
